question: Which third-party indexers support Stellar, and which should I pick if I need historical cross-account filtering?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer derives its named examples and status claims from the current official Stellar documentation, then dates the roster so it is not presented as permanent. As of 2026-07-11, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

You need an **indexer** rather than RPC alone for historical cross-account filtering: RPC event history is bounded and Horizon does not provide arbitrary cross-account filtered indexes ([Indexers Overview](https://developers.stellar.org/docs/data/indexers)).

Established Stellar choices include **SubQuery**, **OBSRVR**, **Goldsky Mirrors/Pipelines**, **OnFinality**, **Mercury**, and **The Graph via Substreams only** (not Stellar Subgraphs or Token API). For deep historical SQL, **Hubble/BigQuery** is another fit.

**Alchemy status requires a dated source note.** As of **2026-07-09**, Alchemy says its indexed Stellar Data API is live on Mainnet and Testnet, with transfer history, account/token balances, and NFT holdings across native, Classic, and Soroban assets; the indexed-API announcement is dated **2026-07-08**. The live Stellar Indexers Overview still says Alchemy was targeted for H1 2026, so that page is stale on this provider; corrective stellar/stellar-docs PR #2573 was still open and unmerged when checked. Treat this as a docs-surface disagreement, not evidence that Alchemy is unavailable.

Selection: use **OBSRVR** for a Stellar-native stream-to-warehouse workflow, **SubQuery** for its multi-chain indexing stack, **Goldsky** if it matches an existing EVM data platform, **Alchemy** if its address-centric indexed APIs cover the needed query, or **Hubble/BigQuery** for SQL-oriented deep history. Verify retention, data model, and current network coverage before committing.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Preserves The Graph's actual boundary: Stellar support is via Substreams, not full Stellar Subgraph or Token API support.
avoid: Do NOT state that Alchemy's Stellar indexed Data API is still merely targeted for H1 2026 without flagging the stale official-docs surface; Alchemy announced the live APIs on 2026-07-08.
Do NOT hide the source disagreement: the provider docs say live while developers.stellar.org still carries the expired roadmap sentence as of 2026-07-09.
Do NOT claim The Graph offers full Subgraph/Token API support for Stellar, or recommend RPC alone for arbitrary historical cross-account filtering.
Do NOT present a changeable current measurement without its provider, scope, and observation date; registered invariants and explicitly historical quantities are not current measurements.
golden.notes:
Re-verified 2026-06-29 against the Indexers Overview: SubQuery, Goldsky (Mirrors), OnFinality, OBSRVR, Mercury support Stellar; The Graph quote confirmed exactly: 'offers Stellar support for Substreams, with no current plans to expand Subgraph or Token API support to Stellar.' Added Space and Time (Q4 2025); Allium (Q1 2026); Alchemy (H1 2026) — freshness-sensitive, retention phrasing matches normalized ~24h default/~7d max. Reward flagging staleness.
Also good if the answer: SubQuery is a safe default (multi-chain, dedicated Stellar starter); OBSRVR is the most Stellar-native; Goldsky Mirrors fit teams already on Goldsky for EVM. | Some providers (e.g. Alchemy, Allium) are launching/expanding Stellar support around 2026 (freshness-sensitive).
Golden cites: developers.stellar.org Indexers Overview page.
CORRECTION 2026-07-09 (todo 901), independently reverified by GT-50 on 2026-07-11: real-world/provider status and docs discoverability disagree. Alchemy's indexed Stellar Data API is confirmed live by its dated announcement and product docs, but the live Stellar Indexers Overview still says targeted H1 2026; PR #2573 remained open/unmerged. Grade the durable behavior: cite a dated provider-status source, keep Alchemy's scope address-centric, and flag the stale docs surface. Do not accept the expired roadmap wording as uncontested current status.

rootCause: Plain freshness drift plus single-surface authoring: the 2026-06-29 review copied a roadmap date from the official Indexers Overview and pinned it instead of gating on a dated provider-status check.
The live developers.stellar.org Indexers Overview is stale for Alchemy; corrective stellar/stellar-docs PR #2573 remained open and unmerged when rechecked 2026-07-09.
